Easy.title: Reverse a String in VB.NET
Easy.content: Write a function in VB.NET that returns the reverse of a given string.
Easy.solution:
Function ReverseString(s As String) As String
  Dim arr As Char() = s.ToCharArray()
  Array.Reverse(arr)
  Return New String(arr)
End Function

Easy.title: Fibonacci Loop in VB.NET
Easy.content: Print the first 10 Fibonacci numbers using a loop in VB.NET.
Easy.solution:
Dim a As Integer = 0, b As Integer = 1
For i As Integer = 0 To 9
  Console.WriteLine(a)
  Dim temp As Integer = a + b
  a = b
  b = temp
Next

             Where n > 10

Hard.title: Interface Implementation in VB.NET
Hard.content: Define an IShape interface with Area() and Perimeter(). Implement it in a Rectangle class.
Hard.solution:
Interface IShape
  Function Area() As Double
  Function Perimeter() As Double
End Interface

Class Rectangle
  Implements IShape
  Public Property Width As Double
  Public Property Height As Double
  Public Function Area() As Double Implements IShape.Area
    Return Width * Height
  End Function
  Public Function Perimeter() As Double Implements IShape.Perimeter
    Return 2 * (Width + Height)
  End Function
End Class

    Return Await client.GetStringAsync(url)
  End Using
End Function

Medium.title: Generic Stack in VB.NET
Medium.content: Implement a generic Stack(Of T) class in VB.NET with Push, Pop, Peek, and IsEmpty.
Medium.solution:
Public Class Stack(Of T)
  Private _items As New List(Of T)()
  Public Sub Push(item As T)
    _items.Add(item)
  End Sub
  Public Function Pop() As T
    Dim last As T = _items(_items.Count - 1)
    _items.RemoveAt(_items.Count - 1)
    Return last
  End Function
  Public Function Peek() As T
    Return _items(_items.Count - 1)
  End Function
  Public ReadOnly Property IsEmpty As Boolean
    Get
      Return _items.Count = 0
    End Get
  End Property
End Class

Medium.title: Delegates and Events in VB.NET
Medium.content: Create a Timer class in VB.NET that fires a Tick event every second. Subscribe a handler that prints a counter.
Medium.solution:
Public Class SimpleTimer
  Public Event Tick()
  Public Sub Start(seconds As Integer)
    For i As Integer = 1 To seconds
      System.Threading.Thread.Sleep(1000)
      RaiseEvent Tick()
    Next
  End Sub
End Class

Dim t As New SimpleTimer()
AddHandler t.Tick, Sub() Console.WriteLine("Tick!")
t.Start(3)

Hard.title: Custom Attributes and Reflection in VB.NET

Hard.solution:
<AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Property)>
Public Class DescriptionAttribute
  Inherits Attribute
  Public Property Text As String
  Public Sub New(text As String)
    Me.Text = text
  End Sub
End Class

For Each prop In GetType(MyClass).GetProperties()
  Dim attr = prop.GetCustomAttribute(Of DescriptionAttribute)()
  If attr IsNot Nothing Then
    Console.WriteLine($"{prop.Name}: {attr.Text}")

Next

Easy.title: Pattern Matching with Select Case
Easy.content: Use VB.NET Select Case with type patterns to handle Integer, String, and Date inputs differently.
Easy.solution:
Sub ProcessValue(value As Object)

    Case Is < 0
      Console.WriteLine("Negative")
    Case 0
      Console.WriteLine("Zero")
    Case 1 To 100
      Console.WriteLine("Positive small")
    Case Else
      Console.WriteLine("Large or other type")

    <Book id="1"><Title>VB.NET Mastery</Title><Price>29.99</Price></Book>
    <Book id="2"><Title>ASP.NET Core</Title><Price>39.99</Price></Book>
  </Catalog>

Dim cheapBooks = From b In catalog.<Book>
                 Where CDec(b.<Price>.Value) < 35

Medium.title: WinForms with MVP Pattern
Medium.content: Implement a WinForms login form using MVP pattern. Separate view, presenter, and model concerns.
Medium.solution:
' ILoginView interface
Public Interface ILoginView
  Property Username As String
  Property Password As String
  Event LoginButtonClicked As EventHandler
  Sub ShowError(msg As String)
End Interface

' LoginPresenter
Public Class LoginPresenter
  Private _view As ILoginView
  Private _model As LoginModel
  Public Sub New(view As ILoginView, model As LoginModel)
    _view = view
    _model = model
    AddHandler _view.LoginButtonClicked, AddressOf OnLogin
  End Sub
  Private Sub OnLogin(s As Object, e As EventArgs)
    If Not _model.Validate(_view.Username, _view.Password) Then
      _view.ShowError("Invalid credentials")
    End If
  End Sub
End Class

Medium.title: Azure Function in VB.NET
Medium.content: Write an Azure Functions HttpTrigger in VB.NET that reads a 'name' query parameter and returns a greeting.
Medium.solution:
Imports Microsoft.Azure.Functions.Worker
Imports Microsoft.Azure.Functions.Worker.Http

Public Class GreetFunction
  <Function("Greet")>
  Public Function Run(<HttpTrigger(AuthorizationLevel.Anonymous, "get")> req As HttpRequestData, executionContext As FunctionContext) As HttpResponseData
    Dim name As String = req.Query("name") ?? "World"
    Dim response = req.CreateResponse()
    response.WriteString($"Hello, {name}!")
    Return response
  End Function
End Class

Hard.title: WPF MVVM with ICommand and INotifyPropertyChanged
Hard.content: Build a WPF MVVM ViewModel in VB.NET with a bound property Counter and an IncrementCommand using ICommand.
Hard.solution:
Public Class MainViewModel
  Implements INotifyPropertyChanged
  Private _counter As Integer
  Public Property Counter As Integer
    Get
      Return _counter
    End Get
    Set(value As Integer)
      _counter = value
      RaiseEvent PropertyChanged(Me, New PropertyChangedEventArgs(NameOf(Counter)))
    End Set
  End Property
  Public Property IncrementCommand As ICommand = New RelayCommand(Sub() Counter += 1)
  Public Event PropertyChanged As PropertyChangedEventHandler Implements INotifyPropertyChanged.PropertyChanged
End Class

Easy.title: Employees Earning More Than Their Managers
Easy.content: Find all employees who earn more than their manager.
Easy.solution:
SELECT e.Name AS Employee
FROM Employee e
JOIN Employee m ON e.ManagerId = m.Id
WHERE e.Salary > m.Salary;

Easy.title: Duplicate Emails
Easy.content: Find all duplicate email addresses in the Person table.
Easy.solution:
SELECT Email
FROM Person
GROUP BY Email
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

Easy.solution:

FROM Customers c
LEFT JOIN Orders o ON c.Id = o.CustomerId
WHERE o.Id IS NULL;

Easy.solution: SELECT * FROM CITY WHERE COUNTRYCODE = 'USA' AND POPULATION > 100000;

Medium.title: Type of Triangle
Medium.content: Write a query that classifies each row in TRIANGLES as Equilateral, Isosceles, Scalene, or Not A Triangle.
Medium.solution:
SELECT CASE
  WHEN A+B<=C OR A+C<=B OR B+C<=A THEN 'Not A Triangle'
  WHEN A=B AND B=C THEN 'Equilateral'
  WHEN A=B OR B=C OR A=C THEN 'Isosceles'
  ELSE 'Scalene'
END FROM TRIANGLES;

Medium.title: Delete Duplicate Emails

Hard.title: Weather Observation Station 5
Hard.content: Query the two cities with the shortest and longest CITY names. On ties, pick alphabetically first/last.

Easy.title: Rising Temperature
Easy.content: Find all dates where the temperature is higher than the previous day.
Easy.solution:
SELECT w1.id
FROM Weather w1
JOIN Weather w2
  ON DATEDIFF(w1.recordDate, w2.recordDate) = 1
WHERE w1.temperature > w2.temperature;

Medium.title: Rank Employees by Salary with Window Functions
Medium.content: Rank all employees by salary within their department using DENSE_RANK().

Medium.title: Cumulative Running Total
Medium.content: Calculate a running total of sales amounts ordered by date.
Medium.solution:
SELECT sale_date, amount,
  SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_total
FROM Sales
ORDER BY sale_date;

Hard.title: Pivot Monthly Sales
Hard.content: Pivot monthly sales data so each month is a column. Use conditional aggregation.
Hard.solution:
SELECT
  product_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN MONTH(sale_date)=1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Jan,
  SUM(CASE WHEN MONTH(sale_date)=2 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Feb,
  SUM(CASE WHEN MONTH(sale_date)=3 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS Mar
FROM Sales
GROUP BY product_id;

Easy.title: Basic Common Table Expression (CTE)
Easy.content: Use a CTE to find all departments with average salary above the company average.
Easy.solution:
WITH dept_avg AS (
  SELECT department_id, AVG(salary) AS avg_sal
  FROM Employees
  GROUP BY department_id
),
company_avg AS (SELECT AVG(salary) AS co_avg FROM Employees)
SELECT department_id, avg_sal
FROM dept_avg, company_avg
WHERE avg_sal > co_avg;

Medium.title: Month-over-Month Growth with LAG()
Medium.content: Calculate month-over-month revenue growth percentage using the LAG window function.
Medium.solution:
SELECT month,
  revenue,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0*(revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 2) AS growth_pct
FROM MonthlyRevenue
ORDER BY month;

Medium.title: Employee Hierarchy with Self Join
Medium.content: List each employee with their manager's name using a self-join.
Medium.solution:
SELECT e.name AS employee, m.name AS manager
FROM Employee e
LEFT JOIN Employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id
ORDER BY e.name;

Hard.title: Recursive CTE for Org Chart
Hard.content: Use a recursive CTE to walk an org-chart from CEO down, outputting each employee's level depth.
Hard.solution:
WITH RECURSIVE org AS (
  SELECT id, name, manager_id, 0 AS depth
  FROM Employee WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, o.depth+1
  FROM Employee e JOIN org o ON e.manager_id=o.id
)
SELECT name, depth FROM org ORDER BY depth, name;

Easy.title: Materialized View Refresh Strategy
Easy.content: Explain when to use a materialized view vs regular view. Write a CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW for daily sales summary.

Medium.title: NTH_VALUE and NTILE Analytics
Medium.content: Assign quartiles to employees by salary using NTILE(4), and find the 2nd highest salary per department with NTH_VALUE.
Medium.solution:
SELECT name, department_id, salary,
  NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY salary) AS quartile,
  NTH_VALUE(salary, 2) OVER (
    PARTITION BY department_id
    ORDER BY salary DESC
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS second_highest
FROM Employee;

Medium.title: Project Employees III
Medium.content: Find the most experienced employee(s) per project.
Medium.solution:
SELECT p.project_id, p.employee_id
FROM Project p
JOIN Employee e ON p.employee_id=e.employee_id
WHERE (p.project_id, e.experience_years) IN (
  SELECT p2.project_id, MAX(e2.experience_years)
  FROM Project p2 JOIN Employee e2 ON p2.employee_id=e2.employee_id
  GROUP BY p2.project_id
);

Hard.title: Game Play Analysis V — Day-1 Retention
Hard.content: For each install date, compute number of installs and Day-1 retention rate.
Hard.solution:
SELECT a1.install_dt,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a1.player_id) AS installs,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT a2.player_id)/COUNT(DISTINCT a1.player_id),2) AS Day1_retention
FROM (
  SELECT player_id, MIN(event_date) AS install_dt FROM Activity GROUP BY player_id
) a1
LEFT JOIN Activity a2
  ON a1.player_id=a2.player_id
  AND DATEDIFF(a2.event_date,a1.install_dt)=1
GROUP BY a1.install_dt;

Easy.title: Index Strategy and EXPLAIN ANALYZE
Easy.content: Identify a missing index for a slow query filtering on (status, created_at). Write the CREATE INDEX statement and interpret EXPLAIN ANALYZE output.
Easy.solution:
-- Before: Seq Scan on orders (cost=0..50000)
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status_created
  ON orders(status, created_at DESC);
-- After EXPLAIN ANALYZE:
-- Index Scan using idx_orders_status_created (cost=0..150 rows=200)
-- Composite index on (status, created_at) supports range queries on created_at after equality on status

Medium.title: Table Partitioning by Range
Medium.content: Create a partitioned sales table partitioned by year range. Create partitions for 2023 and 2024.

Hard.title: Hopper Company Queries II
Hard.content: For each month, compute the percentage of working drivers who accepted at least one ride.
Hard.solution:
WITH months AS (
  SELECT generate_series(1,12) AS month
),
active_drivers AS (
  SELECT d.driver_id,
    MONTH(d.join_date) AS join_month
  FROM Drivers d WHERE YEAR(d.join_date)=2020
)
SELECT m.month,
  ROUND(
    IFNULL(
      SUM(CASE WHEN r.requested_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)*100.0
      / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT ad.driver_id),0)
    ,0)
  ,2) AS working_percentage
FROM months m
LEFT JOIN active_drivers ad ON ad.join_month <= m.month
LEFT JOIN AcceptedRides r
  ON r.driver_id=ad.driver_id AND MONTH(r.requested_at)=m.month
GROUP BY m.month;

Hard.title: Find the Subtasks That Did Not Execute
Hard.content: Find subtasks that were not executed using a recursive CTE.
Hard.solution:
WITH RECURSIVE sub AS (
  SELECT task_id, subtasks_count AS sub_id FROM Tasks
  UNION ALL
  SELECT task_id, sub_id-1 FROM sub WHERE sub_id>1
)
SELECT s.task_id, s.sub_id AS subtask_id
FROM sub s
LEFT JOIN Executed e
  ON s.task_id=e.task_id AND s.sub_id=e.subtask_id
WHERE e.subtask_id IS NULL;

Medium.title: updateOne with $set and $inc
Medium.content: For user with email 'alice@example.com', set status to 'active' and increment loginCount by 1.

Medium.title: Query with $gt, $lt, $in
Medium.content: Find all products with price between 10 and 100, and category in ['Books', 'Toys'].

Hard.title: bulkWrite — Mixed Operations
Hard.content: Use bulkWrite to insert one user, update another's email, and delete a third in a single call.

Easy.title: Projection — Include/Exclude Fields
Easy.content: Find all users, returning only name and email fields, excluding _id.
Easy.solution: db.users.find({}, { name: 1, email: 1, _id: 0 });

Medium.title: Aggregation Pipeline — Group and Count
Medium.content: Use the aggregation pipeline to count the number of products per category, sorted by count descending.

Medium.title: $lookup — Join Two Collections
Medium.content: Join orders with users using $lookup to embed the user document into each order.

Hard.title: Full-Text Search with Text Index
Hard.content: Create a text index on the 'description' field of products. Search for documents containing 'wireless bluetooth'.

Easy.title: Array Query Operators — $elemMatch
Easy.content: Find all orders where at least one item has quantity > 5 and price < 20 using $elemMatch.

Medium.title: $facet — Multi-Pipeline Aggregation
Medium.content: Use $facet to simultaneously compute total count, average price, and price range buckets for a products collection.

Medium.title: Multi-Document ACID Transaction
Medium.content: Transfer 100 credits from account A to account B using a multi-document transaction with commit/abort.

Hard.title: Change Streams — Watch for Inserts
Hard.content: Open a change stream on the 'orders' collection that reacts to new inserts and prints the inserted document.

Easy.title: Atlas Search Autocomplete Index
Easy.content: Create an Atlas Search index with autocomplete on the 'name' field. Write a search query with autocomplete.

Medium.title: Schema Validation with $jsonSchema
Medium.content: Create a 'users' collection with JSON Schema validation requiring name (string), email (string), age (int >= 18).

Medium.title: Time Series Collection
Medium.content: Create a time series collection for IoT sensor data with timestamp, sensorId metadata, and temperature.

Hard.title: $graphLookup — Recursive Graph Traversal
Hard.content: Use $graphLookup to traverse an employee reporting hierarchy and find all reports under a given manager.

Easy.title: Atlas Vector Search — Semantic Query
Easy.content: Create a vector search index on a 'plot_embedding' field (1536 dimensions). Run a $vectorSearch query to find top 5 similar movies.

Medium.title: Sharding a Collection by Hash Key
Medium.content: Enable sharding on the 'ecommerce' database. Shard the 'orders' collection by hashed userId for even distribution.

Hard.title: Oplog Tailing for Custom CDC
Hard.content: Tail the MongoDB oplog to implement a basic Change Data Capture (CDC) pipeline that streams insert/update operations to a processing queue.

Easy.title: Create Table with Constraints
Easy.content: Create a 'users' table with id (SERIAL PRIMARY KEY), name (VARCHAR NOT NULL), email (UNIQUE), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()).

Easy.title: INSERT … RETURNING
Easy.content: Insert a new user and return the generated id and created_at in a single statement.

Easy.title: Case-Insensitive Search with ILIKE
Easy.content: Find all users whose name starts with 'ali' (case-insensitive).
Easy.solution: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name ILIKE 'ali%';

Medium.title: UPSERT with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE
Medium.content: Insert a user or update email if the email already exists.
Medium.solution:
INSERT INTO users (name, email)
VALUES ('Bob', 'bob@example.com')
ON CONFLICT (email)
DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name, updated_at = NOW();

Medium.title: Query JSONB Fields
Medium.content: Given a 'products' table with a JSONB 'metadata' column, find all products where metadata->>'brand' = 'Sony'.
Medium.solution:
SELECT * FROM products
WHERE metadata->>'brand' = 'Sony';

Hard.title: Array Columns and UNNEST
Hard.content: A 'posts' table has a tags TEXT[] column. Find all posts tagged 'postgresql'. Unnest tags to count tag frequency.
Hard.solution:
-- Posts with 'postgresql' tag:
SELECT * FROM posts WHERE 'postgresql' = ANY(tags);

-- Tag frequency:
SELECT tag, COUNT(*) AS frequency
FROM posts, UNNEST(tags) AS tag
GROUP BY tag
ORDER BY frequency DESC;

Easy.title: ROW_NUMBER and RANK Window Functions
Easy.content: Assign row numbers and dense ranks to employees ordered by salary descending within each department.
Easy.solution:
SELECT name, department_id, salary,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary DESC) AS row_num,
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary DESC) AS dense_rnk
FROM employees;

Medium.title: Stored Function with PL/pgSQL
Medium.content: Write a PL/pgSQL function get_discount(price NUMERIC) that returns 10% discount for price > 100, else 5%.

Medium.title: Audit Trigger with NEW and OLD
Medium.content: Create an audit trigger that logs every UPDATE on 'orders' to an 'orders_audit' table with old/new values and timestamp.

Hard.title: Full-Text Search with tsvector and GIN Index
Hard.content: Add a tsvector column to 'articles', populate it, create a GIN index, and search for 'postgres performance'.

Easy.title: Recursive CTE — Fibonacci
Easy.content: Use a recursive CTE to generate the first 10 Fibonacci numbers in PostgreSQL.

Medium.title: LATERAL JOIN for Top-N per Group
Medium.content: Use a LATERAL join to get the top 3 orders by amount for each customer.
Medium.solution:
SELECT c.name, o.id, o.amount
FROM customers c
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT id, amount
  FROM orders
  WHERE customer_id = c.id
  ORDER BY amount DESC
  LIMIT 3
) o;

Medium.title: Partitioned Table by Range
Medium.content: Create a sales table partitioned by year. Add partitions for 2023 and 2024 with default partition.

Hard.title: Advisory Locks for Distributed Locking
Hard.content: Use pg_try_advisory_lock to implement a non-blocking distributed lock identified by a job ID. Release on completion.

Easy.title: EXPLAIN ANALYZE — Read Query Plans
Easy.content: Use EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on a query and describe what a Seq Scan vs Index Scan means, and when to prefer each.
Easy.solution:
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42;

-- Seq Scan: reads all rows — prefer when fetching > ~10% of table
-- Index Scan: follows B-tree/hash index — prefer for selective queries (few rows)
-- Bitmap Index Scan: batches index lookups — prefer for moderate selectivity
-- Buffers: shows shared_hits (cached) vs reads (disk) for tuning

Medium.title: Logical Replication — Publication and Subscription
Medium.content: Set up logical replication from a primary (publisher) to a replica (subscriber) for the 'orders' table.

Medium.title: Row-Level Security (RLS)
Medium.content: Enable RLS on a 'documents' table. Create a policy that restricts each user to see only their own rows.

Hard.title: Custom Aggregate Function
Hard.content: Create a custom aggregate function that computes the geometric mean of a set of positive numbers.

Easy.title: pgvector — Semantic Similarity Search
Easy.content: Install pgvector extension. Create a 'documents' table with a vector(1536) embedding column and perform cosine similarity search.

Medium.title: Foreign Data Wrapper (postgres_fdw)
Medium.content: Use postgres_fdw to create a foreign table mapping to a remote PostgreSQL database's 'orders' table.

Hard.content: Write a minimal PostgreSQL C extension that exposes a SQL function 'add_two(int, int) RETURNS int'.

FileUtils.copyFile(src, new File("screenshots/failure.png"));

Easy.content: Add @Epic, @Feature, @Story Allure annotations to a TestNG test. Attach a screenshot on failure.

Medium.content: Use AShot library to capture a full-page screenshot and compare it to a baseline with a 1% pixel diff tolerance.

Hard.title: Selenium Tests in GitHub Actions CI
Hard.content: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs a Maven Selenium TestNG suite headlessly and uploads the Allure report as an artifact.
Hard.solution:
# .github/workflows/selenium.yml
name: Selenium Tests
on: [push]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - name: Install Chrome
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y google-chrome-stable
      - name: Run Tests
        run: mvn test -Dheadless=true
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          name: allure-results
          path: target/allure-results

Easy.title: Setup ChromeDriver and Open URL
Easy.content: Initialize a Chrome WebDriver using webdriver-manager, navigate to https://example.com, print the title, and quit.
Easy.solution:
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.service import Service
from webdriver_manager.chrome import ChromeDriverManager

driver = webdriver.Chrome(service=Service(ChromeDriverManager().install()))
driver.get('https://example.com')
print(driver.title)
driver.quit()

Easy.title: Find Element by ID and Click
Easy.content: Find a button by id 'submit-btn' and click it.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
btn = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'submit-btn')
btn.click()

Easy.title: Type into Input Field
Easy.content: Find an input by name 'username', clear it, type 'testuser'.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
input_el = driver.find_element(By.NAME, 'username')
input_el.clear()
input_el.send_keys('testuser')

Medium.title: Explicit Wait with WebDriverWait
Medium.content: Wait up to 10 seconds for a button 'load-more' to be clickable, then click it.
Medium.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait
from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By

wait = WebDriverWait(driver, 10)
btn = wait.until(EC.element_to_be_clickable((By.ID, 'load-more')))
btn.click()

Medium.title: Page Object Model in Python
Medium.content: Create a LoginPage class encapsulating username, password fields and login button. Add a login() method.

Hard.title: Pytest Fixture for WebDriver
Hard.content: Write a pytest fixture that initializes a headless ChromeDriver, yields it to the test, and quits after.
Hard.solution:
import pytest
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options

@pytest.fixture
def driver():
  opts = Options()
  opts.add_argument('--headless')
  opts.add_argument('--no-sandbox')
  opts.add_argument('--disable-dev-shm-usage')
  d = webdriver.Chrome(options=opts)
  yield d
  d.quit()

Easy.title: Select from Dropdown
Easy.content: Select 'Canada' from a <select> dropdown with id 'country'.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import Select
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
select = Select(driver.find_element(By.ID, 'country'))
select.select_by_visible_text('Canada')

Easy.title: Wait for URL Change
Easy.content: After clicking submit, wait up to 10s for URL to change.
Easy.solution:
current = driver.current_url
driver.find_element(By.ID, 'submit').click()
WebDriverWait(driver, 10).until(EC.url_changes(current))

Medium.title: Switch to New Browser Tab
Medium.content: Click a link that opens a new tab, switch to it, print its URL, then close and return to original.
Medium.solution:
original = driver.current_window_handle
driver.find_element(By.ID, 'open-tab').click()
for handle in driver.window_handles:
  if handle != original:
    driver.switch_to.window(handle)
print(driver.current_url)
driver.close()
driver.switch_to.window(original)

Medium.title: Iterate and Select Radio Buttons
Medium.content: Find all radio buttons name='size'. Print their values. Select the one with value 'medium'.
Medium.solution:
radios = driver.find_elements(By.CSS_SELECTOR, "input[name='size']")
for r in radios:
  print(r.get_attribute('value'))
next(r for r in radios if r.get_attribute('value') == 'medium').click()

Hard.title: Handle JavaScript Alerts and Prompts
Hard.content: Trigger a JS alert, accept it. Trigger a JS prompt, type 'hello', and confirm. Trigger a confirmation dialog and dismiss it.
Hard.solution:
# Alert
driver.execute_script("alert('Test alert')")
driver.switch_to.alert.accept()

# Prompt
driver.execute_script("prompt('Enter something:','')")
alert = driver.switch_to.alert
alert.send_keys('hello')
alert.accept()

# Confirmation
driver.execute_script("confirm('Are you sure?')")
driver.switch_to.alert.dismiss()

Easy.title: Drag and Drop with ActionChains
Easy.content: Drag element id='source' and drop on element id='target'.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.common.action_chains import ActionChains
source = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'source')
target = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'target')
ActionChains(driver).drag_and_drop(source, target).perform()

Medium.title: Click Hidden Button via JavaScript
Medium.content: Click a display:none button using execute_script.
Medium.solution:
btn = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'hidden-btn')
driver.execute_script('arguments[0].click();', btn)

Medium.title: File Upload via sendKeys
Medium.content: Upload test_file.pdf to an input[type=file] element with id 'upload'.
Medium.solution:
import os
file_path = os.path.abspath('test_file.pdf')
driver.find_element(By.ID, 'upload').send_keys(file_path)

Medium.title: Screenshot on Failure with pytest
Medium.content: Write an autouse pytest fixture that captures a screenshot when a test fails.

Hard.title: Wait for Staleness and Re-find Element
Hard.content: Click reload button. Wait for old element to go stale, then re-find it.
Hard.solution:
old = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'result')
driver.find_element(By.ID, 'reload-btn').click()
WebDriverWait(driver, 10).until(EC.staleness_of(old))
new_el = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'result')

Easy.title: Remote WebDriver with Selenium Grid
Easy.content: Connect to a Selenium Grid 4 at http://grid:4444 using webdriver.Remote with ChromeOptions.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver import Remote
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options

driver = Remote(
  command_executor='http://grid:4444',
  options=Options()
)
driver.get('https://example.com')
driver.quit()

Medium.title: CDP — Block Image Requests
Medium.content: Use Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) to block all image URL patterns during a test.

Medium.title: Parallel Tests with pytest-xdist
Medium.content: Run pytest suite in parallel across 4 workers. Each worker gets its own driver.
Medium.solution:
# conftest.py
@pytest.fixture(scope='function')
def driver():
  d = webdriver.Chrome()
  yield d
  d.quit()

# Run: pytest -n 4 tests/

Medium.title: Capture Browser Console Errors
Medium.content: After loading a page, retrieve all SEVERE browser console logs.
Medium.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
opts = Options()
opts.set_capability('goog:loggingPrefs', {'browser': 'ALL'})
driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=opts)
driver.get('https://example.com')
errors = [e for e in driver.get_log('browser') if e['level'] == 'SEVERE']
print(errors)

Hard.title: Custom FluentWait with Lambda
Hard.content: Poll every 500ms for 20s until a table has more than 10 rows.

Easy.title: Selenium 4 Relative Locators
Easy.content: Find the input directly above the Submit button using Selenium 4 relative locators.
Easy.solution:
from selenium.webdriver.support.relative_locator import locate_with

submit = driver.find_element(By.XPATH, '//button[text()="Submit"]')
inp = driver.find_element(locate_with(By.TAG_NAME, 'input').above(submit))
inp.send_keys('test')

Medium.title: CDP Device Emulation — iPhone 12
Medium.content: Use CDP to emulate iPhone 12 (390x844, deviceScaleFactor=3, mobile=True).

Medium.title: Visual Regression with Pillow
Medium.content: Capture a screenshot of element id='header'. Compare pixel-by-pixel with a baseline. Assert similarity > 99%.
Medium.solution:
from PIL import Image, ImageChops
import io

el = driver.find_element(By.ID, 'header')
curr = Image.open(io.BytesIO(el.screenshot_as_png))
baseline = Image.open('baseline.png')
diff = ImageChops.difference(curr, baseline)
changed = sum(1 for p in diff.getdata() if p != (0,0,0,0))
assert (1 - changed / (curr.width * curr.height)) * 100 > 99

Hard.title: GitHub Actions CI with Headless Selenium
Hard.content: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that installs dependencies, runs headless pytest, and uploads the HTML report.
Hard.solution:
# .github/workflows/test.yml
name: Tests
on: [push]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v4
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: pytest --html=report.html --self-contained-html
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          name: report
          path: report.html

Hard.content: Use pytest-httpserver to mock /api/users endpoint. Run Selenium against a page that fetches from it.

Hard.title: Allure Report Integration with pytest
Hard.content: Add @allure.feature, @allure.story, allure.step to a pytest login test. Generate Allure report.
